Start at developer.zendesk.com, then visit relevant Zendesk official docs pages and pricing pages as needed.

raw_result.description: Customer service and helpdesk platform with APIs for ticketing, help center, chat, talk, sell, and related Zendesk products.
raw_result.auth_notes: Zendesk's security/auth docs describe OAuth access tokens and API token authentication, and also document basic authentication with email address and password for some APIs, while noting password access may be disabled depending on account settings.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing shows API access as a developer-tools feature on paid Zendesk plans; API volume and higher limits are tied to specific paid plans/add-ons.

api_surface.breadth: Official developer docs include broad REST API coverage across Support, Help Center, Talk, Chat, Sell and other Zendesk products, and also document GraphQL via Sunshine Conversations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Zendesk page in the gathered evidence documents an MCP server; absent first-party evidence, treated as not documented.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Zendesk documents plan-based rate limits. The rate-limits page shows Support and Help Center API limits tied to Zendesk Suite plan levels, and mentions higher built-in limits such as 2500 requests/minute on top enterprise tiers plus endpoint-specific limits and rate-limit headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Strong first-party developer portal, dedicated auth and rate-limit docs, broad multi-product API reference, and pricing page explicitly exposing API access and additional API volume as plan features.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field api_surface.mcp_exists is not directly supported by affirmative first-party evidence from the gathered pages; the current false value is based on lack of documentation in the reviewed official pages rather than an explicit official statement of nonexistence.
